block.text: Configure your Mantisnet App environment

If your Splunk administrator has provided a different index for you to use than the default main index , type the index name or select from the list.Clearing the input field displays the full list of indexes on this Splunk instance only.If your indexes are in a remote cluster , you will have to type the index name.

Scripted inputs are used to populate local CSV lookup files.The following lookup scripts will be enabled when you submit this setup page :

Public DNS Nameservers : public_dns_lookup.py

You can get your data into the app via Kafka, HTTP Event Collector or Raw TCP.

If you wish to use Kafka , then complete the Kafka settings below and the Kafka Modular Input stanzas will enable automatically when you submit the setup page (unless the Splunk index is a Search Head only).

If you wish to use HTTP Event Collector or Raw TCP then select this checkbox. Then, via the App's Navigation menu you can browse to the Data Inputs setup page. Ensure that you use the correct sourcetype (as documented on the App's landing page) and index that you have configured above.

input.label: Using HTTP Event Collector or Raw TCP ?

block.text: These settings are used globally across all Kafka Stanzas

Kafka Bootstrap Server(s)

Single value or Comma delimited list of servers ie: somehost:9092,anotherhost:9092

Kafka Username

Leave blank if you don't require authentication.

Kafka Password

The password you enter here for the above username will be securely encrypted in local/passwords.conf

All kafka logs get written to SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/mantisnet_app_kafka_modinput.log and are searchable in Splunk.This log level applys globally to all your Kafka stanzas.You can also browse to the Data Inputs page and set a specific log level for each stanza if you wish.
